WASHINGTON, May 14.-Ho We!
Ho ye! Ho We! Your Uncle Samuel
hath three towns to sell! The same
will be put up at auction at date
soon to be announced!


Irrigation! Oasis town lots! All
ready to blossom like the rose. Co.
ing. going! going


1t is something entirely new. 1t
was made legal by an act of con-
gress approved April 16, 1906.
Towns laid out in the new irriga-
ton projects may now be sold at
auction. And, in the same act the
government is given the right to
furnish, under lease, unlimited pow
er to private individuals fer manu-
featuring purposes. The first auc-
ton will occur in a few weeks.
Three towns are to be put up for
sale, and they are out in Idaho, on
the Snake river. Theey have come
into existence within the past Il
months. A year ago there was
nothing there but sand. sage brush
coyotes and rattlesnakes. There
was not a habitation within 81
miles. Now there are banks, stores
school houses and churches. AL
is due to the magic of government
irrigation.


The towns are Hepburn, Scnerre
and Rupert.


On the map look for the southern
boundary of idaho. Bordering thi
line and just about the middle of
is Lincoln county. In the southeas
corner Of the county is the Min
doka irrigation project, which cor
sists in retaining 10000 acres c
desert by daming the Snake rive
and diverting the water to tn
lands. The three towns hav
sprung up along the newly bull
railroad.
